title: task to goal rollup percent goal_verify
purpose: C3.4 defines task to goal rollup percent goal verify for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery.
scope: Owns `C3.4` only; siblings under `C3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §5 — Branch C — Product execution plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#5-branch-c-product-execution-plane).
behavior_steps: When platform promotion reaches L5, [D4.6](D4.6-platform-work-pack-fragment-export.md) pack-fragment-export distills stabilized L0–L4 artifacts from consumer repos into `template-packs/` fragments—default pipelines, role bindings, verify suites, and catalog components new goals inherit at company instantiation.

L5 fragments ship organizational capital upward from product promotions, not one-off edits in a single branch; pack authors validate against Plane F pack schema before publish and catalog INDEX marks discoverability per [D6.1](D6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md).

New goals at H1 pull active template-pack fragments through [B5.1](B5.1-active-role-from-template-pack.md) role bindings and [C1.4](C1.4-pack-defined-pipelines-per-company-template.md) pipeline registry—the conductor preflight loads inherited verify suites without re-deriving defaults in chat.

L5 promotion closes only when [D6.4](D6.4-platform-done-staleness-node-wired.md) artifact-graph wiring notifies downstream goals of fragment changes and dual-write records promotion_queue completion to journal and state.json.

If a fragment bypasses pack schema validation, forks without [D5.3](D5.3-fork-new-catalog-entry-provenance.md) provenance, or omits staleness graph wiring, platform pursuit fails closed at H2 until pack-fragment-export reconciles and restores invariant bindings.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

narrative: C3.4 defines task to goal rollup percent goal verify for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery. In practice, the system when platform promotion reaches l5, [d4.6](d4.6-platform-work-pack-fragment-export.md) pack-fragment-export distills stabilized l0–l4 artifacts from consumer repos into `template-packs/` fragments—default pipelines, role bindings, verify suites, and catalog components new goals inherit at company instantiation, then l5 fragments ship organizational capital upward from product promotions, not one-off edits in a single branch; pack authors validate against plane f pack schema before publish and catalog index marks discoverability per [d6.1](d6.1-platform-done-catalog-index-row.md).
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-c 2026-06-28 -->

Task completion rolls up to goal percent complete and goal_verify prerequisites: queue empty, all required evidence passed, conformance green. Rollup is S0 where scripts aggregate task state from state.json and journal mirror.

False rollup—marking goal ready while tasks lack evidence—is a Plane G failure class. Dashboard may show percent for operators; pursuit uses machine checks only.
